basics.name: Ardalan Aryashad
basics.label: Ph.D. Student in Mechanical Engineering & M.Sc. Student in Computer Science
basics.summary: Ph.D. student at the USC IMPACT Lab working on vision-language-action models, robotic task planning, and agentic AI for model-based systems engineering.

work.name: IMPACT Laboratory, University of Southern California
work.summary: Research on vision-language-action models and agentic AI for mechanical systems engineering.
highlights: Developed a graph-based planning framework (VLAG) for VLA models in long-horizon manipulation tasks.
Trained a CLIP-based vision model achieving 95% accuracy on environmental key state prediction.
Fine-tuned a sentence transformer model reaching 98% instruction-to-task matching accuracy.
Trained a Transformer-based action model validated on the CALVIN dataset.
Designed single and multi-agent workflows for MBSE modeler tasks.
Created a Visual Paradigm plugin to auto-generate SysML diagrams using a custom LLM agent.
Implemented a text extraction pipeline integrated into a RAG system for an AI agent.

work.name: Micro-Nano Systems Lab, Sharif University of Technology
work.summary: Control system design for a PCR microsystem.
highlights: Conducted system identification experiments on a PCR microsystem.
Designed a 2-DOF PID controller reducing settling time by 17% and eliminating overshoot.
Trained a neural network predictive model and implemented it in MPC.
Executed MPC on a Raspberry Pi using MATLAB/Simulink.

work.name: Experimental Solid Mechanics and Manufacturing Lab, Sharif University of Technology
work.summary: Pipe material selection and surge pressure simulation.
highlights: Reviewed literature on essential parameters for pipe material selection in water networks.
Conducted surveys and interviews with design engineers to rank design parameters.
Simulated surge pressure phenomena using FEM in Iranian PE pipes.

work.name: Automotive Industries Research & Innovation Center of SAIPA (AIRIC)
highlights: Researched and simulated regenerative braking performance for Saipa Shahin.
Developed a script to convert Google Maps data into simulation-compatible road profiles.
Simulated regenerative braking across multiple real-world routes.

work.name: Industrial Saipa Heavy Dies Manufacturing
work.summary: Maintenance department support and process automation.
highlights: Developed an Excel-based preventive maintenance schedule.
Built a web-based maintenance request system to improve department workflow.

volunteer.organization: IDETC-CIE, WACV, International Journal of Computer Integrated Manufacturing
volunteer.summary: Reviewing manuscripts in robotics, computer vision, and mechanical engineering.

volunteer.organization: Mechanical Engineering Department, Sharif University of Technology
volunteer.summary: Applied Finite Element, Control Design, Mechanical Design, Embedded Systems, Dynamics, and Statics courses.

volunteer.organization: IMPACT Laboratory, University of Southern California
volunteer.summary: Mentoring students on RAG modules, hyperparameter optimization for robotic manipulation, and dataset collection.

awards.summary: Fellowship awarded to incoming Ph.D. students in the AME department.

awards.title: Best Teaching Assistant in Mechanical Engineering

awards.title: National University Entrance Exam — Ranked 146th out of 144,000
awards.awarder: Iran National Organization for Educational Testing
awards.summary: Mathematics branch, national rank 146 out of 144,000 participants.

publications.name: VLAG: Graph-Based Planning for Vision-Language-Action Models in Long Horizon Manipulation Tasks
publications.summary: A modular graph-based planning framework for VLA models that decomposes long-horizon tasks into structured subtasks using dedicated vision, language, and action modules.

publications.name: From Filters to VLMs: Benchmarking Defogging Methods through Object Detection and Segmentation Performance
publications.publisher: Workshop on Image/Video/Audio Quality Assessment, WACV 2026
publications.summary: A benchmark pipeline evaluating fog-removal methods by their effect on downstream object detection and segmentation tasks across synthetic and real-world fog datasets.

references.name: Prof. Yan Jin
references.reference: Advisor, IMPACT Laboratory, University of Southern California. Available upon request.
